tested.name: Coding sparsity (native firing statistics of a property)

concept.name: Coding sparsity (native firing statistics of a property)
concept.definition: For a probed property P, take its best linear-probe direction d_P and compute the distribution of d_P·activation over a token corpus. Coding sparsity κ(P) = heavy-tailedness of that distribution (excess kurtosis, or Gini/L0 of thresholded activations, or 1 − firing-rate). High κ = P fires rarely with heavy tails (matches an SAE's L1/L0 sparsity prior); low κ = P is dense/near-Gaussian (mismatches the prior, so the SAE splits or absorbs it). Measurable from probes alone, before training the SAE.
concept.prediction: SAE-atom recovery of P — max cosine between any trained atom and d_P — increases monotonically with κ(P). Concrete computable split: properties below median κ get max-cosine <0.5 (SAE misses them, exactly the class that 'fails to beat probes' in arXiv:2502.16681); properties above median κ get max-cosine >0.8. Falsified if recovery is flat or non-monotone in κ.
concept.reduces_to: Superposition sparsity + feature splitting/absorption (Chanin et al. 2024). Risk: 'how sparse is the property's activation' renamed. Earns keep as an a-priori per-property scalar that predicts SAE recoverability instead of a post-hoc failure label.

test.design: Preregistered before running (file: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/prereg_kappa_sae.md). Operationalization: κ(P) = -log2(f_P) where f_P = corpus firing rate of token-level property P; d_P = normalized mean-difference direction at gpt2-small blocks.8.hook_resid_pre (train half of tokens); Recovery(P) = max cosine between d_P and any of the 24576 decoder atoms of the cached pretrained jbloom GPT2-Small SAE (same hook). 12 candidate properties (punctuation, capitals, numerics, 'the', be-forms, brackets, pronouns, months, ALL-CAPS, sentence-start, years, comma) on ~19,200 tokens of local English prose; drop props with <50 positives or held-out probe AUC<0.7. DECISION RULE fixed in advance — Primary: Spearman ρ(κ, Recovery); SUPPORTED if ρ≥+0.6 with permutation p<0.05; REFUTED if ρ≤+0.2; inconclusive between. Secondary (the prediction's own concrete split): below-median-κ props must have maxcos<0.5 AND above-median >0.8; concrete version REFUTED if >30% of props violate their side. Robustness: same pipeline rerun at layer 6 with the layer-6 SAE.
test.numbers: Layer 8 (primary, n=10 surviving properties): Spearman ρ=0.418, permutation p=0.117 — fails the preregistered support bar (ρ≥0.6, p<0.05) but sits above the flat-refute bar. Concrete split: violation rate 0.80 (>>0.30 refute threshold) — ALL 5 above-median-κ properties fail maxcos>0.8 (observed 0.55–0.67; NO property anywhere exceeds 0.72), and 3/5 below-median fail <0.5. Explicit non-monotonicity: the sparsest property (pronouns, κ=7.37) has maxcos=0.55, LOWER than the much denser 'the' (κ=4.67, maxcos=0.72). Layer 6 robustness replicates everything: ρ=0.455, p=0.093, violation rate 0.80, max observed maxcos=0.70. Per property (L8): punct κ=2.51/mc=0.46, capital 3.60/0.39, sentstart 4.29/0.52, the 4.67/0.72, allcaps 4.74/0.56, numeric 5.22/0.56, beforms 5.43/0.67, brackets 5.72/0.66, comma 5.96/0.55, pronoun 7.37/0.55. Verdict: the concrete quantitative prediction (monotone increase with the <0.5 / >0.8 median split) is refuted — recovery saturates in a narrow 0.4–0.7 band nearly flat in κ; only a weak positive trend (ρ≈0.42–0.46, not significant at n=10) survives. Caveats: n=10 properties, one model, technical-prose corpus (SAE trained on OpenWebText), mean-difference d_P is one specific choice of property direction; 'survived/refuted' here applies to the stated computable split, not every conceivable κ formalization. Scripts/results: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/{kappa_sae.py,result.json,result_L6.json}.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, so the refutation is real, not a broken run: Oracle A (a raw SAE decoder atom fed in as d_P) returned maxcos=1.0000 at both layers — the cosine machinery and normalization are correct (the exact check that would have caught the earlier .norm bug). Random-direction baseline maxcos=0.135, so observed 0.4–0.7 values are far above noise and the metric has dynamic range. Oracle B: every surviving d_P separates held-out positives from negatives with ROC-AUC 0.91–0.99 (mean-diff directions are genuinely property-encoding); 2 properties (months, years) were dropped for <50 positives per the preregistered rule, none dropped for AUC. All cosines verified in [-1,1].

tested.name: Carrier rank (representational dimensionality of a property)

concept.name: Carrier rank (representational dimensionality of a property)
concept.definition: For property P, carrier rank ρ(P) = number of orthogonal probe directions needed to reach 95% of the maximum achievable probe accuracy, measured by iterative nullspace projection (INLP) or the participation ratio of between-class mean differences. ρ=1 means P genuinely lives on a single direction (the 'one direction' assumption of SAEs holds); ρ>1 means P is a subspace/polytope that no single atom can capture. Computed from probes, before the SAE.
concept.prediction: The count of SAE atoms significantly aligned with P (cosine>0.3) grows with ρ(P), while single-atom max-cosine recovery falls as ρ grows. Concrete: ρ=1 properties have exactly one dominant matching atom (cos>0.8); ρ≥3 properties have no atom above 0.5 and instead spread across ≥ρ atoms (measured feature-splitting count ≈ ρ). Falsified if split-count is independent of ρ, or if high-ρ properties are still captured by a single atom.
concept.reduces_to: Feature splitting and multi-dimensional/manifold features (Engels et al. 2024). Risk of renaming 'feature splitting'. Earns keep as an a-priori geometric scalar predicting how many atoms a property fragments into.

test.design: Model: gpt2-small CPU, layer-7 resid_pre, trained public SAE (jbloom/GPT2-Small-SAEs-Reformatted, 24576 atoms, 2907 live on corpus). Ground-truth ρ constructed by UNIONS of 5 base concepts (dog/war/love/music/snow, 6 prompts each + 6 neutral): 5 properties at ρ=1, 3 at ρ=2, 2 at ρ=3. Property direction v_P = unit diff-in-means (pooled prompts vs neutral); atom alignment = |cos(W_dec row, v_P)| over live atoms; secondary metric = projection norm onto the ρ-dim component subspace. Preregistered BEFORE running (PREREG.md, committed): validity gates V1 (pairwise concept |cos|<0.5) and V2 (ρ=3 participation ratio ≥2.0); oracles O1/O2; decision rule — REFUTED if spearman(ρ,count03)≤0.3 OR mean count03(ρ=3)≤mean(ρ=1) [split-count independent of ρ] OR any ρ=3 maxcos≥0.8 [captured by single atom]; SUPPORTED if spearman(ρ,count03)≥0.6 AND spearman(ρ,maxcos)≤−0.3 AND no F2. Run 1 tripped the validity gates (raw diff-means share a big "topic vs neutral" component, cos 0.47–0.63; ρ=3 PR≈1.9) → INCONCLUSIVE_DESIGN as prereg'd. Amendment prereg'd BEFORE run 2: center concept diffs by their cross-concept mean; decision rule unchanged.
test.numbers: Run 2 (valid: pairwise cos 0.11–0.42, ρ=3 PR 2.57/2.64): spearman(ρ, count03) = 0.063 (needed >0.3 to survive); mean count03 ρ=1 = 2.6 vs ρ=3 = 2.5 — split-count flat in ρ. spearman(ρ, maxcos) = −0.47 (that half is directionally right). Concrete claims: ρ=1 properties' maxcos = 0.32–0.43 — NO atom above 0.5 anywhere, let alone the predicted single dominant >0.8 (0/5); ρ=3 maxcos 0.31/0.34 (<0.5 half holds trivially). Secondary subspace-projection count03 DID grow with ρ (means: 2.6 → 15.3 → 19), so a subspace reformulation of the concept might survive — but the prediction as stated (cosine-to-property counts, dominant single atom at ρ=1) fails on both falsification-relevant counts. Verdict fired via preregistered F1.
test.sanity: O1 pipeline oracle: a planted live SAE atom fed through the identical pipeline returns maxcos = 1.000 (>0.99 required). O2 relevance oracle: null distribution from 200 random unit directions gives 99.5th-pct max|cos| = 0.171; all 5 concept directions score 0.32–0.43, i.e. 2–2.5x above null — the measurement genuinely detects SAE alignment, so the flat split-count is a real result, not a broken meter. Run 1 additionally proved the gates work: they caught a real design flaw (shared topic component) before any verdict was issued.

backlogLeft: Legibility coordinate (auto-interp fidelity as its own axis)
